revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Futami Okitama Shrine]][[es:Santuario Futami Okitama]][[fr:Futami Okitama-jinja]][[ja:二見興玉神社]][[th:ศาลเจ้าฟูตามิโอกิตามะ]]


{{Short description|Japanese Shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building
| name                  = Santuario Futami Okitama
| building_name         = 二見興玉神社
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Futamiokitama jinja Haiden.jpg
| deity                 ={{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|qid=Q17227983|1=Okitama-no-Kami}} 
| municipality          = [[:en:Ise (Mie)|Ise]]
| country               = {{JPN}}
| image_size            = 270px
| prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|34.50895|N|136.78782|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}

The {{Nihongo|'''Futami Okitama shrine'''|二見興玉神社|Futami Okitama-jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Futami-ura|Futami-ura]], part of the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami Okitama Shrine |url=https://japancheapo.com/place/futami-okitama-shrine/ |access-date=28 September 2021 |publisher=japancheapo.com |language=en}}</ref> It is known for its proximity to the [[Meoto Iwa]] rocks, which serve as ''[[torii]]'' gates for believers offering prayers to the sun.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami Okitama Jinja（Wedded Rocks) |url=https://www.iseshima-kanko.jp/en/see-and-do/1292 |access-date=28 September 2021 |publisher=Organización de Convenciones de Turismo de Ise Shima |language=en}}</ref>

== Characteristics ==

The shrine is dedicated to the Shinto deity Sarutahiko Ōkami and worships the Meoto Iwa rocks, which represent the gods [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}. Because of its proximity, 700 m from the coast, it is common for couples to go to the shrine to pray for their marriage.<ref>{{Cite web |title=La opinión de Petit Futé sobre SANCTUAIRE FUTAMI OKITAMA (MEOTO-IWA) |url=https://www.petitfute.es/v75397-ise/c1173-visites-points-d-interet/c925-edifice-religieux/c930-sanctuaire-lieu-de-pelerinage/1951276-sanctuaire-futami-okitama-meoto-iwa.html |access-date=28 September 2021}}</ref> The temple is a typical purification stop before visiting the [[Ise Grand Shrine]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami Okitama Shrine |url=https://livejapan.com/en/in-kansai/in-pref-mie/in-ise_toba_shima/spot-lj0009289/ |access-date=28 September 2021 |publisher=Live Japan |language=en}}</ref> through the ceremony of ''{{ill|Harae|en|Harae|qid=Q3127244|lt=Harae|1=Harae}}'',<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami-okitama Shrine |url=https://thegate12.com/spot/1428 |access-date=28 September 2021 |language=en}}</ref> pilgrims used to bathe in the waters of Futami and then go to the neighboring shrine..<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami Okitama Shrine |url=https://www.ana.co.jp/en/us/japan-travel-planner/mie/0000001.html |access-date=28 September 2021 |language=en}}</ref>

The grounds of the enclosure are decorated with numerous statues of frogs.;<ref>{{Cite web |title=Meotoiwa (The Wedded Rocks) |url=https://www.kintetsu.co.jp/foreign/english/sightseeing/mie/spot17.html |access-date=28 September 2021 |language=en}}</ref> According to the beliefs, these attract people and objects back.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Meoto Iwa (Wedded Rocks) |url=https://www.japan-guide.com/e/e4303.html |access-date=28 September 2021 |publisher=japan-guide.com |language=en}}</ref> The faithful whose prayers were fulfilled donate the figures of amphibians.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Futami Okitama-jinja Shrine (二見興玉神社) |url=https://japantravel.navitime.com/en/area/jp/spot/02301-2300034/ |access-date=28 September 2021 |language=en}}</ref>

== See also ==

* [[Japanese mythology]]
* [[Kuniumi]]
